meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Level Up Your Freelance SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Master API integration with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-impact, professional SaaS solutions for clients.
content.answer_block: Stop building static interfaces. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent API orchestration, you can transform basic CRUD apps into dynamic, data-driven powerhouses. This workflow automates the boilerplate, enforces robust error handling, and accelerates your development cycle, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS features in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The freelance graveyard is littered with 'basic' apps—simple CRUD wrappers that provide no real value to clients. You’ve been there: you build a clean UI, but the moment a client asks to sync data with a third-party CRM or process live payments, your architecture crumbles. You hit the wall of manual integration, and suddenly, your profit margin evaporates into endless debugging sessions.

It is time to stop playing small. Real-world SaaS success isn't about writing more code; it's about building smarter pipelines. By integrating robust APIs into your stack, you move from being a 'frontend builder' to a 'systems architect.' This guide shows you how to use modern AI-driven tooling to bridge the gap between basic prototypes and enterprise-ready integrations.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, formalize your data requirements. Use Antigravity to generate a schema that ensures type safety between your app and the external service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript schema definition for a REST API integration. Include interfaces for request payloads, error handling structures, and success responses based on the provided OpenAPI specification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate type definitions for api_schema.json'

steps.title: Scaffold the Service Layer
steps.description: Decouple your API logic from your components. This prevents spaghetti code and makes future API updates a breeze.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean service layer class in TypeScript that implements the defined schema, includes an axios instance with interceptors for auth, and handles retries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create api-service.ts with retry logic and bearer token auth'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Error Handling
steps.description: Don't let your app crash on a 401 or 503 error. Build a global error handler that guides the user and logs the issue for you.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current API service to include a centralized error utility that maps status codes to user-friendly notifications and logs to Sentry.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor api-service.ts to include centralized error handling middleware'

steps.title: Connect Data to UI Components
steps.description: Use React Query or SWR to manage server state. This gives you caching and loading states for free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook using TanStack Query that consumes the API service and manages loading, error, and data states for the client dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate use-api-data custom hook with tanstack query integration'

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Ensure your integration stays stable. Generate automated tests that mock the API responses to verify your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vitest suite for the new API service, using MSW to mock server responses for success, timeout, and authorization error scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for api-service.ts using msw and vitest'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated API gateway layer; never call third-party endpoints directly from your UI components to protect your API keys.
Implement 'optimistic updates' in your UI to make your app feel instantaneous, even when the API call is still pending.
Use request memoization for GET endpoints to prevent redundant network calls during rapid component re-renders.
Always log the correlation ID from your API responses on the frontend; it is the fastest way to debug issues with your client's support team.
Use environment-based base URLs to ensure your staging and production environments never cross-pollinate data.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is live, focus on setting up CI/CD pipelines to automatically run your new test suite on every commit, ensuring your client's app never breaks in production.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop building basic apps and start engineering professional SaaS ecosystems. Stop fighting your tools and start mastering the workflow that separates top-tier freelancers from the rest. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
